title: Implement Daily Ops Brief — full vertical slice
context:
Implement an automated 8am daily summary delivered to the assistant chat thread. It aggregates: today's Google Calendar blocks, unread priority emails, GitHub blocker issues, and top 3 agent tasks. Full design: docs/daily-ops-brief-design.md.

Existing services to integrate with:
- app/services/google_calendar.py → GoogleCalendarService(db).get_rafe_schedule(days=1)
- app/services/email_service.py   → EmailService(db).get_unread(max_results=20)
- app/services/github_sync.py     → subprocess(['gh', 'issue', 'list', ...]) pattern
- Task model (app/models.py)       → direct DB query, no external dep

The RoutineRunner (app/orchestrator/routine_runner.py + engine.py) supports named hooks on a cron schedule. The chat persistence+broadcast pattern is in app/routers/chat.py.
acceptance: 1. GET /api/brief/today returns 200 with JSON: {markdown, sections: [{name, icon, items, error, available}], generated_at}. 2. GET /api/brief/today?send_to_chat=true also persists a ChatMessage(role='assistant', metadata={source:'daily_brief'}) to the configured session and broadcasts via WebSocket. 3. The 'daily_ops_brief' hook is registered in engine.py's RoutineRunner hooks dict. 4. A RoutineRegistry row is seeded on startup with name='daily_ops_brief', hook='daily_ops_brief', schedule='0 13 * * *' (13:00 UTC = 8am ET standard), execution_policy='auto', enabled=True. 5. Each data section degrades gracefully: any failed adapter sets section.error and section.available=False — never raises — the other sections still render. 6. Unit tests: assemble() with all sources OK, assemble() with each source failing independently, format_markdown() with full data, format_markdown() with empty/errored sections. 7. Integration tests: GET /brief/today returns 200, send_to_chat=true creates ChatMessage in DB, unauthenticated request returns 401.

tasks.title: Create app/services/brief_service.py
tasks.notes:
Implement three core things:

1) Dataclasses:
   @dataclass BriefItem(source, title, detail, priority='normal', url=None, time=None)
   @dataclass BriefSection(name, icon, items: list[BriefItem], error=None, available=True)
   @dataclass DailyBrief(generated_at, sections: list[BriefSection], suggested_plan)

2) Four adapters — each is an async method that MUST return BriefSection, never raise:
   - CalendarAdapter: GoogleCalendarService(db).get_rafe_schedule(days=1); filter events where date == today ET; fallback to ScheduledEvent table if configured returns nothing or throws
   - EmailAdapter: EmailService(db).get_unread(max_results=20); filter/score by keywords: 'urgent', 'ASAP', 'blocker', 'critical', 'action required'; return top 5 sorted by recency
   - GitHubAdapter: asyncio subprocess(['gh','issue','list','--state=open','--label', GITHUB_BLOCKER_LABEL,'--json','number,title,url,updatedAt','--limit=10'], timeout=15s); GITHUB_BLOCKER_LABEL env var defaults to 'blocker'; catch FileNotFoundError (gh not installed)
   - TasksAdapter: DB query — SELECT from Task WHERE status IN ('inbox','active') ORDER BY sort_order ASC, created_at ASC LIMIT 5. NOTE: no priority_score column — use sort_order.

3) BriefService.generate(db) → runs all 4 adapters via asyncio.gather (with asyncio.wait_for per adapter, timeout=10s), returns DailyBrief.
   BriefFormatter.to_markdown(brief: DailyBrief) → markdown string matching the design doc example
   BriefFormatter.suggest_plan(brief) → 1-3 sentence heuristic (GitHub blockers > high tasks > meetings)

Every adapter wraps its logic in try/except Exception — on error: return BriefSection(name=..., icon=..., items=[], error=str(e), available=False).

tasks.title: Create app/routers/brief.py
tasks.notes:
Two endpoints:
  GET /brief/today?send_to_chat=false
  - Requires Bearer auth (Depends(require_auth))
  - Calls BriefService(db).generate(db), BriefFormatter.to_markdown(brief)
  - Returns: {markdown: str, sections: [...], generated_at: iso_str}
  - If send_to_chat=true:
    * session_key = os.getenv('BRIEF_CHAT_SESSION_KEY', 'main')
    * Ensure session exists (see ensure_session_exists pattern in chat.py)
    * Write ChatMessage(role='assistant', content=markdown, metadata={'source':'daily_brief','generated_at':iso_str}) to DB
    * Broadcast via manager.broadcast_to_session(session_key, {type:'message', data:{...}})
    * Return includes message_id

Auth: use Depends(require_auth) from app.auth.

tasks.title: Register router in app/main.py
tasks.notes: Import and include brief router — follow same pattern as all other routers. Prefix /api/brief.

tasks.title: Register 'daily_ops_brief' hook in engine.py RoutineRunner
tasks.notes:
In engine.py, around line 357 where RoutineRunner is instantiated with a hooks dict:
  Add: 'daily_ops_brief': _make_daily_brief_hook(db)

Implement _make_daily_brief_hook as a module-level function that returns an async hook function:
  async def _hook(routine: RoutineRegistry):
    from app.services.brief_service import BriefService, BriefFormatter
    import os
    brief = await BriefService(db).generate(db)
    markdown = BriefFormatter.to_markdown(brief)
    session_key = os.getenv('BRIEF_CHAT_SESSION_KEY', 'main')
    # post to chat using same pattern as brief.py send_to_chat=true path
    return {'status': 'ok', 'sections': len(brief.sections)}
  Wrap entire body in try/except — log [BRIEF] prefix — never raise.

tasks.title: Seed RoutineRegistry row for daily brief
tasks.notes:
On engine startup (or via a migration script), INSERT OR IGNORE a RoutineRegistry row with:
  - id: uuid4 (str)
  - name: 'daily_ops_brief'  ← this is the unique identifier (not 'key')
  - description: 'Daily Ops Brief — 8am summary of calendar, email, GitHub blockers, and top tasks'
  - hook: 'daily_ops_brief'  ← maps to RoutineRunner hooks dict key
  - schedule: '0 13 * * *'   ← 13:00 UTC = 8:00am ET standard time. NOTE: compute_next_fire_time() does NOT use schedule_timezone — cron is always interpreted as UTC by the scheduler
  - schedule_timezone: 'UTC' (not 'America/New_York' — the timezone field exists in the model but is not consumed by compute_next_fire_time() in app/orchestrator/scheduler.py)
  - execution_policy: 'auto'
  - enabled: True
  - policy_tier: 'standard'
  Use INSERT OR IGNORE or SELECT then INSERT to be idempotent.
  Can be done as a migration in migrations/ OR as a startup seed in engine._run_startup_seeds() if that exists, OR directly in engine startup.

tasks.title: Write tests
tasks.notes:
tests/test_brief_service.py:
  1. Unit: BriefFormatter.to_markdown() with fully populated DailyBrief (all 4 sections, items present) — assert all expected emoji headers appear
  2. Unit: BriefFormatter.to_markdown() with all sections errored (available=False, error='service unavailable') — assert brief still renders without raising
  3. Unit: TasksAdapter.fetch() with mock AsyncSession returning 3 Task rows — assert 3 BriefItems, section.error is None
  4. Integration: GET /api/brief/today with TestClient, mock BriefService.generate to return a fixed DailyBrief — assert 200, response has 'markdown' key
  5. Integration: GET /api/brief/today without auth token — assert 401

Follow conftest.py patterns already in tests/.
